shortAnswer: CRP (C-reactive protein) is an inflammation marker that rises during infections or inflammatory conditions in the body. Normal values are below 5 mg/l. Elevated levels can indicate an infection or inflammation. Seek medical attention promptly if levels are significantly elevated.
metaDescription: CRP elevated? Normal ranges, causes of high C-reactive protein, and when to see a doctor — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Should this value be rechecked in 2 weeks?
Could a chronic inflammation be present?
pageTitle: CRP value — C-Reactive Protein explained | Medyra
